explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Documentation implies mechanisms for oversight. A pilot project description references a structured approach to AI deployment, including defined business criteria for customer eligibility. It also references future plans for expanding AI insights.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The "Public Service Enterprise Group: AI Use Cases 2024" document provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The article's description of AI tools used for analysis and feedback, as well as for instant information retrieval, implies mechanisms for oversight and clarity in how AI is employed and its outputs are presented.
relevant_sources.title: Public Service Enterprise Group: AI Use Cases 2024

relevant_sources.summary: This pilot project description provides evidence for **governance**, **oversight**, and **transparency**. Governance is supported by the structured approach to AI deployment, including defined business criteria for customer eligibility and future plans for expanding AI insights. Oversight is demonstrated through the description of a feedback mechanism and ongoing evaluation of AI-driven alerts. Transparency is implied by the mention of AI analysis and personalized breakdowns of energy usage by appliance.
relevant_sources.title: Pilot Project #2: Bidgely Next Generation Insights - 2020 Scope

oversight.findings: A pilot project description describes a feedback mechanism for oversight. It also describes ongoing evaluation of AI-driven alerts.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: Documentation describes AI tools used for analysis, feedback, and instant information retrieval, implying clarity in how AI is employed and its outputs are presented. A pilot project description further implies transparency through AI analysis and personalized breakdowns of energy usage by appliance.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

Governance is supported by the structured approach to AI deployment, including defined business criteria for customer eligibility and future plans for expanding AI insights. Oversight is demonstrated through the description of a feedback mechanism and ongoing evaluation of AI-driven alerts. summary.overall_findings: Public Service Enterprise (PSEG)'s disclosures describe AI tools used for analysis and feedback, as well as for instant information retrieval, demonstrating practices related to transparency. Additionally, a pilot project description outlines a feedback mechanism for oversight and references a structured approach to AI deployment, addressing governance. Overall, 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars have documented evidence, with transparency, oversight, and governance addressed at the policy level. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 2 additional pillars. This assessment is based on a review of 8 publicly available sources.
